name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
summary: Marjorie Taylor Greene has consistently demonstrated a pattern of promoting conspiracy theories, bigotry, and violence. She has supported calls to execute prominent Democratic politicians, promoted Islamophobic, antisemitic, and white supremacist views, and amplified conspiracy theories about mass shootings, the Clinton family, and 9/11. Greene has also compared COVID-19 safety measures to the persecution of Jews during the Holocaust, equated the Democratic Party with Nazis, and praised Vladimir Putin. Her behavior has been deemed incendiary enough to warrant removal from committee assignments and expulsion from the Freedom Caucus. Greene's actions and rhetoric show a disregard for facts, civility, and human life, and have earned her widespread criticism and condemnation.

entries.fact: Marjorie Taylor Greene supported calls to execute prominent Democratic Party politicians, including Hillary Clinton and Barack Obama.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Islamophobic, antisemitic, and white supremacist views, including the white genocide conspiracy theory.

Marjorie Taylor Greene amplified conspiracy theories that allege government involvement in mass shootings in the United States, implicate the Clinton family in murder, and suggest the attacks of 9/11 were a hoax.
Marjorie Taylor Greene equated the Democratic Party with Nazis and compared COVID-19 safety measures to the persecution of Jews during the Holocaust.
Marjorie Taylor Greene later apologized for comparing COVID-19 safety measures to the persecution of Jews during the Holocaust.
The U.S. House of Representatives voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from all committee roles in response to her endorsements of political violence.
Eleven Republicans joined unanimous Democrats in the vote to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from all committee roles.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Russian propaganda and praised Vladimir Putin during the Russian invasion of Ukraine.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was appointed to new committee roles.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the conservative House Freedom Caucus after insulting fellow caucus member Congresswoman Lauren Boebert.
Marjorie Taylor Greene unsuccessfully attempted to oust Mike Johnson from his role as Speaker of the House of Representatives.
Marjorie Taylor Greene posted death threats against Democrats and bigotry directed at the family of Barack Obama as a moderator of the Family America Project's Facebook group.

Marjorie Taylor Greene visited the U.S. Capitol and congressional offices, where she called for Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez to 'face the American citizens that you serve' and told her to 'get rid of [her] diaper'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene referred to Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez's office as a 'day care center'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that Representatives Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib were not 'official' representatives because they were sworn in to Congress on the Quran.
Marjorie Taylor Greene accused Representatives Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib of supporting Islamic law and said they should be sworn in on the Bible instead of the Quran.

Marjorie Taylor Greene posted a video on Facebook where she held an AR-15 style rifle and warned antifa groups to 'stay the hell out of Northwest Georgia', which was later taken down by Facebook for violating its terms of service.

Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a gun rights rally in Villanow hosted by Silent No Longer, a group campaigning to make Walker County a Second Amendment sanctuary, and posed for photos with Chester Doles, a 5th-generation Ku Klux Klansman.

Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a meme on Facebook depicting herself holding an AR-15 style rifle next to a collage of pictures of Ocasio-Cortez, Omar, and Tlaib, with the caption 'Squad's worst nightmare', which was later deleted by Facebook for violating its policies on inciting violence.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's company, Taylor Commercial, received $182,300 in Paycheck Protection Program funding from the Small Business Administration during the COVID-19 pandemic, and around two months later, Greene donated $450,000 to her campaign.

Marjorie Taylor Greene appeared at a gun rights rally in Ringgold, Georgia, where the Georgia III% Martyrs provided security, and she called Nancy Pelosi 'anti-American', a 'hypocrite', and a 'bitch' whom 'we're going to kick ... out of Congress'
Marjorie Taylor Greene's opponent, Kevin Van Ausdal, withdrew from the race, leaving Greene unopposed in the general election

Marjorie Taylor Greene made deceptive claims that her husband Perry's Floyd County voting record revealed voter fraud, despite the Floyd County chief elections clerk stating that nothing happened to the missing ballot and it was canceled according to procedure
Marjorie Taylor Greene wore a face mask onto the House floor that read 'Trump Won', despite Trump having lost the 2020 U.S. presidential election to Joe Biden
Marjorie Taylor Greene raised an objection to counting Michigan's electoral votes during the counting of electoral votes, which was rejected because it was not signed by a member of the U.S. Senate

The Federal Election Commission asked Marjorie Taylor Greene's 2022 re-election campaign treasurer to provide information about $3.5 million of unitemized campaign contributions from undisclosed donors received in the first half of 2021
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a face mask while sheltering in place during the riot at the US Capitol.
Marjorie Taylor Greene tweeted that Democrats must be held accountable for the political violence inspired by their rhetoric during the debate to impeach Trump.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely suggested that the rioters at the Capitol were members of antifa dressed as Trump supporters in a series of tweets.

Marjorie Taylor Greene retweeted a clip from Fox News host Tucker Carlson claiming that agents of the Federal Bureau of Investigation might have organized and participated in the attack on the Capitol.
Marjorie Taylor Greene indicated her support for defunding Planned Parenthood in an interview with Fox News.

Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote on Twitter that children should not wear masks, calling recommendations by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and other public health officials 'unhealthy for their psychological, emotional, and educational growth' and 'emasculating' for boys.
Marjorie Taylor Greene asked Trump to issue her a pardon for her efforts to overturn the 2020 election.
Marjorie Taylor Greene perpetuated the Biden–Ukraine conspiracy theory alleging that Biden had been bought off by foreign interests in an interview with Greg Kelly of Newsmax.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that the Plan B contraceptive 'kills a baby in the womb', when in fact it prevents ovulation and thus prevents pregnancy.
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a mask in a secured room with other members of Congress during the storming of the Capitol, despite the COVID-19 pandemic.
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed articles of impeachment against Joe Biden, citing alleged abuse of power as the reason for impeachment.
Representative Jimmy Gomez announced he had drafted a resolution to expel Marjorie Taylor Greene from the House after reports that she had previously called for violence against Democrats.

Nancy Pelosi spoke of an 'enemy within the House of Representatives' and the need to increase security measures, alluding to Marjorie Taylor Greene and other Republican representatives who wanted to bring guns onto the House floor.
Representatives Nikema Williams and Sara Jacobs introduced a resolution to censure Marjorie Taylor Greene for making threatening comments to her colleagues and to call for her resignation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was removed from all committee assignments due to incendiary remarks she had made before her election.
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to get a COVID-19 vaccine, stating there was no reason to because she is 'perfectly healthy'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that COVID-19 vaccine passports might be 'Biden's mark of the beast', a reference to the Book of Revelation, echoing a far-right conspiracy theory that getting vaccinated is equivalent to pledging allegiance to the devil.
Representative Jimmy Gomez formally introduced his resolution to expel Marjorie Taylor Greene, with the support of 72 Democrats and no Republicans.

Marjorie Taylor Greene and Lauren Boebert cast two lone votes against a bill to reauthorize the National Marrow Donor Program, with Greene falsely claiming that the bill could fund aborted fetal tissue.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against the January 6 commission, a proposed commission which would have investigated the attack on the Capitol.

Marjorie Taylor Greene called for justice for Ashli Babbitt, a Trump supporter who was fatally shot by a Capitol police officer when she stormed the Capitol and attempted to break into the House chamber.

Marjorie Taylor Greene and Matt Gaetz initiated a nationwide 'America First Tour', beginning in The Villages, Florida, where they repeated unsubstantiated claims of fraud purported to have occurred during the 2020 election.

Marjorie Taylor Greene aggressively confronted Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez outside the House chamber, loudly asking why she supported antifa and Black Lives Matter, which Greene falsely characterized as terrorists.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was fined $500 for not wearing a mask on the House floor.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of 21 House Republicans to vote against a resolution to give the Congressional Gold Medal to police officers who defended the U.S. Capitol on January 6.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promised that companies that complied with the House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack's request to retain phone records would be 'shut down'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a conference hosted by white supremacist Nick Fuentes, where Fuentes called the January 6 attack 'awesome' and praised Putin and Hitler.
Marjorie Taylor Greene had introduced five different resolutions to impeach Biden by September 2022.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the Freedom Caucus by a vote of its members after calling fellow caucus member Lauren Boebert a 'little bitch' during an argument on the House floor.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a pair of resolutions to expunge both of Trump's impeachments.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a pair of resolutions to expunge the impeachments of Trump for a second time.
Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized Trump's support for Israeli strikes against Iran and opposed the possible involvement of the United States in the war.

Marjorie Taylor Greene began supporting the House of Representatives' discharge petition that would release the files from the Jeffrey Epstein client list and criticized Trump for actively opposing the database's release.
Marjorie Taylor Greene announced that she will resign from her seat in the U.S. House of Representatives, with the resignation going into effect on January 5, 2026.

Marjorie Taylor Greene opposed foreign aid, saying that her 'regular, normal person, normal, everyday American values' dictate that 'our hard-earned tax dollars should just go for America, not for... China, Russia, the Middle East, Guam – whatever, wherever'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called for the results of the 2020 U.S. presidential election in Georgia to be decertified.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced an article of impeachment against Biden, with no co-sponsors.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a measure condemning the Myanmar coup d'état.
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared House mask mandates to the Holocaust, saying that they are 'exactly the [same] type of abuse' as Jews being 'put in trains and taken to gas chambers in Nazi Germany'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted to repeal the Authorization for Use of Military Force Against Iraq Resolution of 2002.

Marjorie Taylor Greene sent Biden a letter calling for an investigation into Fauci over his statements on the origin of SARS-CoV-2, the virus that causes COVID-19, and called COVID-19 a 'manufactured plague' caused by a 'Chinese-made virus'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Biden's suggestion to provide door-to-door vaccinations to 'medical brownshirts' and said: 'You can't force people to be part of the human experiment'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene publicly apologized for her comments comparing mask mandates and vaccine policies to the Holocaust after a private visit to the Holocaust Museum in Washington, D.C.
Marjorie Taylor Greene dismissed the COVID-19 variants, including the Delta variant, saying, 'no one cares'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene posted misinformation about the virus, saying it 'is not dangerous for non-obese people and those under 65'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene stated that she would kick out every Chinese person in the United States who is loyal to the Chinese Communist Party and impose strict tariffs on China if she had the authority to do so.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against the bipartisan ALLIES Act, which would increase the number of special immigrant visas for Afghan allies of the U.S. military.

Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that Biden would be 'sending one of his police state friends to your front door' to inquire whether they had received a vaccine, and said, 'in the South, we all love our Second Amendment rights, and we're not really big on strangers showing up on our front door'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene admitted that COVID-19 was spreading, but falsely said that hospitals were not overwhelmed with afflicted patients, and added, 'we're human, we can't live forever, we're going to catch all kinds of diseases and illnesses and other viruses'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene purchased shares of Digital World Acquisition Corp., a special purpose acquisition company that had just merged with Trump Media & Technology Group for the purpose of funding Trump's planned social media app, Truth Social.
Marjorie Taylor Greene had been fined 20 times for not wearing a mask on the House floor, totaling $48,000 in fines.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the anti-vaccine film Died Suddenly, which promotes misinformation about COVID-19 vaccines and Great Reset conspiracy theories.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted in favor of H.Con.Res. 21, which directed President Biden to remove U.S. troops from Syria within 180 days.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Russian propaganda, including claims that Ukraine is a Nazi state, and praised Vladimir Putin.
Marjorie Taylor Greene stated that lax security at the Mexico–United States border contributed to the opioid epidemic in the United States.
Marjorie Taylor Greene sponsored a resolution to censure Democratic U.S. representative Rashida Tlaib for alleged 'antisemitic activity' and 'sympathizing' with Hamas.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called for the lifting of U.S. sanctions on Russia.
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed an amendment to a House foreign aid bill on Ukraine that would require all members of the House voting in favor of the bill to enlist in the Ukrainian military.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was called 'Moscow Marjorie' by former Republican congressman Ken Buck due to her promotion of Russian propaganda.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of 18 Republicans to vote against admitting Sweden and Finland to NATO.
Marjorie Taylor Greene repeatedly refused to address Fauci as Doctor, questioned his medical licence and accused him of covering up COVID-19's origins during a congressional hearing.
Marjorie Taylor Greene became the first Republican to call the situation in Gaza a 'genocide'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed a defense appropriations amendment that would end U.S. funding of Israel's Iron Dome.
Marjorie Taylor Greene broke with her party over the federal government shutdown, disagreeing over a proposed extension of Obamacare subsidies.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg's efforts were funded by billionaire George Soros.
Marjorie Taylor Greene confronted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg near the U.S. Capitol, questioning his gun control activism and calling him a 'coward' for ignoring her.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said Nancy Pelosi is guilty of treason and that treason is a crime punishable by death in a Facebook video.
Marjorie Taylor Greene visited Nancy Pelosi's office and suggested that Pelosi would either be killed or imprisoned for treason in Facebook live-streamed videos.
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Black Lives Matter activists to white nationalist participants at the August 2017 Unite the Right rally in Charlottesville, Virginia
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that Muslims who believe in Sharia law should not be in the U.S. government and contended that the Democratic Party is holding Black Americans as 'slaves'
Marjorie Taylor Greene told viewers that 'the only way you get your freedoms back is it's earned with the price of blood' in an interview with gun activist Chris Dorr.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a bill to abolish the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives.
Marjorie Taylor Greene denounced the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act, a civil rights and police reform bill, as an 'Anti-Police bill'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that she would 'love to trade' far-right British political commentator Katie Hopkins 'for some of our white people here that have no appreciation for our country'
Marjorie Taylor Greene called the Squad supporters of 'terrorism' and 'Hamas' who 'don't belong in Congress' and referred to them as the 'Jihad Squad'
Marjorie Taylor Greene was detailed to have support for and past ties with extremist militia groups, including the Three Percenters and the Oath Keepers, by The New York Times.
Marjorie Taylor Greene co-sponsored the Old Glory Only Bill, a bill to ban U.S. embassies from flying pride flags.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said she was 'censored' by Twitter for 'absolutely believing with all my heart that God's creation is he created them male and female'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene tried to block the Equality Act while it was being debated on the House floor, saying that it would 'destroy God's creation' and 'violate everything we hold dear in God's creation'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed replacing the Equality Act with a bill that would exempt nonprofit organizations, allow people to sue the federal government 'if their religious rights are violated', and prevent trans women and girls from participating in women's sports.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the verdict in the Derek Chauvin trial was a result of jury intimidation by Black Lives Matter, which she compared to the Ku Klux Klan

Marjorie Taylor Greene and other House Republicans were interested in launching an America First Caucus, which believes that 'America is a nation with a border, and a culture, strengthened by a common respect for uniquely Anglo-Saxon political traditions'
Marjorie Taylor Greene's office said that the new America First caucus would be launched 'very soon'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called transgender women 'men playing dress up' and 'literally having an identity crisis', saying, 'They never will be [women], and I refuse to recognize them that way.'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that schools teaching about LGBT people is 'mental/emotional child abuse'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the July 4 Highland Park parade shooting was 'designed to make Republicans go along with more gun control' because the shooting occurred 'in a rich, white neighborhood'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that any Republican who supported the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act was 'a traitor to our party, a traitor to their voters and a traitor to our donors'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene attended the America First Political Action Conference, which was criticized by Senate minority leader Mitch McConnell for having 'white supremacists or anti-Semitism'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized Ketanji Brown Jackson for not answering Senator Marsha Blackburn's question 'what is a woman?'
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that straight people would go extinct within less than two centuries due to LGBT-inclusive educators, calling them 'trans terrorists'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that an entire pride month celebrating LGBT people 'needs to end'
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced the 'Protect Children's Innocence Act', which would make giving gender-affirming care to transgender youth a felony punishable by up to 25 years in prison
Marjorie Taylor Greene responded to California state senator Scott Wiener by saying that the Protect Children's Innocence Act would stop 'communist groomers' such as Wiener
An updated version of Marjorie Taylor Greene's 'Protect Children's Innocence Act' passed the House by a vote of 216-211
Marjorie Taylor Greene speculated that the Pizzagate conspiracy theory is real.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed doubt that the perpetrator of the 2017 Las Vegas shooting acted alone, suggesting it was an attack on the right to bear arms.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the August 2017 Charlottesville white nationalist rally was an 'inside job'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's Facebook account expressed support for the execution of leading Democrats, including Pelosi, Barack Obama, and Hillary Clinton, as well as support for the execution of FBI agents.
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested John F. Kennedy Jr.'s death in a plane crash in 1999 was a 'Clinton murder' because he was a possible rival to her for a U.S. Senate election in New York.

Marjorie Taylor Greene agreed with a conspiracy theory known as Frazzledrip, which asserts that there is a video of Hillary Clinton and her assistant Huma Abedin murdering a child in a satanic ritual and that Clinton later ordered a hit on a police officer to cover it up.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed support for a conspiracy theory that a plane did not hit the Pentagon during the September 11 attacks.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 9/11 was part of a U.S. government plot.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2018 Stoneman Douglas High School shooting in Parkland, Florida, was an organized false flag operation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2012 Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a false flag operation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote that Nancy Pelosi tells Hillary Clinton several times a month that 'we need another school shooting' in order to persuade the public to want strict gun control.
Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a video promoting the white genocide conspiracy theory, which claims that Zionists are conspiring to flood Europe with migrants to replace the native white populations.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted a conspiracy theory that George Soros' family collaborated with the Nazis in Hungary and is 'trying to continue what was not finished'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's Facebook account responded to a post asking about hanging leading Democrats, saying 'Stage is being set. Players are being put in place. We must be patient. This must be done perfectly or liberal judges would let them off.'
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg had been replaced in public appearances by a body double.
Marjorie Taylor Greene followed and taunted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg, accusing him of using children for his cause.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called David Hogg an 'idiot' who is trained 'like a dog'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed racist, antisemitic, and Islamophobic views in videos published by Politico.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that Democratic National Committee staff member Seth Rich was murdered by the MS-13 gang on Obama's behalf.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that Obama is secretly a Muslim.

Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted a false anti-Semitic conspiracy theory that the 2018 Camp Fire, a deadly wildfire in California, may have been caused by 'what looked like lasers or beams of blue light' from 'space solar generators' in a scheme involving California Governor Jerry Brown, companies PG&E, Rothschild & Co, and Solaren.
Marjorie Taylor Greene does not accept the scientific consensus of evolution, calling it a 'type of so-called science' and saying: 'I don't believe in evolution. I believe in God'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called David Hogg '#littleHitler'.
A staffer for Marjorie Taylor Greene threatened to have a reporter arrested after the reporter tried to ask Greene a question about her harassment of David Hogg.
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Representative Maxine Waters had also committed treason.

Marjorie Taylor Greene advocated a 'national divorce' between red states and blue states, and suggested that red states disenfranchise people who move there from blue states for a period of five years.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the conspiracy theory that the U.S. government is planning to force Americans to eat fake meat grown in a 'peach tree dish' by Bill Gates.

Marjorie Taylor Greene said that 'People die in the cold. This Earth warming, and carbon, is actually healthy for us. It helps us to feed people, it keeps people alive. ... We need to hold Democrats accountable and defund all of their climate garbage'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene said that Democrats had designed the killing of Ayman al-Zawahiri to boost Biden in the midterms because the Russian invasion of Ukraine had not achieved this, shortly before doubting he had been killed.

Marjorie Taylor Greene posted online a map of hurricane affected areas with an overlay of electoral map by political party, and wrote that 'Yes they can control the weather … It's ridiculous for anyone to lie and say it can't be done'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's Facebook account shared a conspiracy theory about the Camp Fire, a deadly Californian wildfire, suggesting that it could have been caused by 'what looked like lasers or beams of blue light' from 'space solar generators' in a scheme involving California governor Jerry Brown, companies PG&E, Rothschild & Co, and Solaren.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was described as a 'conspiracy theorist' by multiple sources, including The Guardian, Associated Press, and Axios, due to her promotion of QAnon conspiracy theories.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's personal Twitter account was locked for 12 hours for multiple violations of Twitter's civic integrity policy, based on her posts including false claims about voting fraud and statements blaming electoral officials in Georgia
Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized Twitter after her account was locked, saying 'Contrary to how highly you think of yourself and your moral platitude, you are not the judge of humanity. God is.'

Marjorie Taylor Greene's behavior was described as 'appalling' by Representative Debbie Wasserman Schultz, who introduced a motion to remove Greene from her committee assignments due to her promotion of conspiracy theories and incendiary statements.

Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell issued a statement attacking 'loony lies and conspiracy theories' as a 'cancer for the Republican Party', which was widely seen as a criticism of Marjorie Taylor Greene.
Marjorie Taylor Greene tweeted that the Republican Party's only problem are 'weak Republicans who only know how to lose gracefully' in response to Mitch McConnell's criticism.
Marco Rubio condemned Marjorie Taylor Greene's suggestion that the Stoneman Douglas High School shooting might have been a false flag, calling it 'deranged or a sadist'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene stated that her social media content did not reflect who she is, prompting a standing ovation from the House Republican Conference.
The full House voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from her committee assignments, with 230 votes in favor and 199 against.
Marjorie Taylor Greene used the word 'retard' to refer to individuals with Down syndrome, and later apologized for the offense caused.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was barred from blocking anyone on her public Twitter or any other social media account while in office and was forced to pay $10,000 to cover legal fees for MeidasTouch
Marjorie Taylor Greene broke Georgia law by claiming homestead exemptions on two properties, when only one may legally be claimed.
Common Cause filed an FEC complaint against Marjorie Taylor Greene for an alleged violation of McCain–Feingold campaign finance provisions.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against rewarding the Capitol Police with the Congressional Gold Medal for defending the Capitol Building during the 2021 United States Capitol attack.
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared herself to a sixth-grader in her understanding of the Holocaust, and her apology was criticized by Jimmy Kimmel as 'as empty as her head'
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the Robb Elementary School shooter had engaged in cross-dressing, echoing similar claims by right-wing politicians and media figures that the shooter was transgender.
Marjorie Taylor Greene's personal Twitter account was permanently suspended for spreading COVID-19 vaccine misinformation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene used a malapropism, calling the police 'gazpacho police', confusing the cold Spanish soup with the Gestapo
Marjorie Taylor Greene accused Republicans who supported then-Supreme Court nominee Ketanji Brown Jackson of being 'pro-pedophile'
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed a federal lawsuit to block a law that allowed a candidate to be removed from a ballot after review by an administrative law judge and the Georgia Secretary of State

Marjorie Taylor Greene testified for three hours in a hearing regarding her eligibility for reelection, after a federal judge denied her challenge to a law that allowed a candidate to be removed from a ballot

Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed without evidence that the Robb Elementary School shooter was in the same Discord server as the teen accused of carrying out the 2022 Buffalo shooting and that there was a third party who 'groomed' both young men.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called a Petri dish a 'peach tree dish' during a livestream
Twitter labeled two tweets by Marjorie Taylor Greene as violating their rules on hateful conduct for misgendering and deadnaming Assistant Secretary for Health Rachel Levine

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the presence of the Georgia Guidestones, a megalithic granite monument, and said that the ecumenical texts inscribed on it represented a nefarious future of 'population control' as envisioned by the 'hard left'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene published a memoir that details her upbringing, her first campaign for Congress, and her three-year tenure as a United States representative.
Marjorie Taylor Greene targeted and misgendered congresswoman Sarah McBride in a series of tweets, labeling her a 'groomer'
Marjorie Taylor Greene celebrated the death of Pope Francis, tweeting 'Today there were major shifts in global leaderships. Evil is being defeated by the hand of God.'
Marjorie Taylor Greene indicated support for executing prominent Democrats

Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted COVID-19 misinformation, birtherism, and other conspiracy theories, including the Pizzagate conspiracy theory and accusations that George Soros is an 'ex-Nazi billionaire' who funds nearly all of 'the Democratic liberal agenda'
Facebook deleted Marjorie Greene's ad, likely due to its content

Marjorie Taylor Greene spread conspiracies about Charlottesville and 'Pizzagate'

Marjorie Greene self-funded her campaign with $450,000 after receiving a Paycheck Protection Program loan, raising concerns about potential corruption
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that her husband's voting record revealed a 'scam'

Marjorie Taylor Greene stated that 'our elections should be decertified', casting doubt on the legitimacy of the 2020 presidential election
